name: Ham Mousse with Peaches
recipeIngredient: Pickled watermelon rind, drained and finely chopped (8 oz from an 8 ounce jar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Combine ham, egg yolks, and mustard in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Process until blended. Add whole eggs one at a time. Add milk, cream, port wine, tomato paste, and pepper; process until completely blended. Taste and add salt if necessary.
3. Pour the mixture into 8 lightly buttered 8-ounce custard cups. Place the cups in a roasting pan lined with double thickness of newspaper. Fill the pan with hot water to come halfway up the sides of the cups. Cover the pan with aluminum foil. Bake in the lower third of the oven until the custards are just set, 35 to 40 minutes. Do not overbake. Remove the custard cups from the pan and cool completely on wire racks.
4. Blanch peaches in boiling water for 1 minute. Cool immediately in ice water. Peel, quarter, and remove pits. Slice thinly.
5. Unmold mousses onto individual serving plates. Garnish with peaches, watermelon rind, and almonds.
